title: grammitis subcapillaris
intro: Grammitis subcapillaris, also known as the False Lip Fern or Green Lip Fern, is a species of fern native to Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Grammitis subcapillaris include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
